dateStatements.label: Translation
dateStatements.notes: The dedication-copy context supports a 1444 terminus ante quem, but the inspected evidence does not establish 1439 as an authorial terminus post quem.
dateStatements.value: By 1444; probably close to production of the 1439-1444 dedication manuscript

entities.description: The anonymous Middle English verse translation of Palladius's De re rustica, including the sixteen-stanza authorial prohemium and thirteen books. This abstract work layer is distinct from physical witnesses, scholarly editions, digital encodings, and manuscript-associated indices.
entities.label: Palladius on Husbondrie (DIMEV 1071 with prohemium DIMEV 254)

rights.basis: The work is directly attested in a 1439-1444 manuscript and the official U-M item containing its text is marked Public Domain / No Copyright-United States. This statement concerns the medieval wording, not manuscript photographs, modern catalog data, or provider interfaces.
rights.component: Fifteenth-century Middle English textual work

dateStatements.label: Composition
dateStatements.value: Fourth century according to the current Bodleian authority heading

entities.description: The late-antique Latin agricultural source work attributed to Rutilius Taurus Aemilianus Palladius. It is the source work translated into Middle English, not a manuscript witness or edition of the Middle English poem.
entities.label: Palladius, De re rustica

bibliographic.citation: Mark Liddell, ed., The Middle-English Translation of Palladius De Re Rustica, Part I—Text (Berlin: E. Ebering, 1896).
bibliographic.title: The Middle-English Translation of Palladius De Re Rustica

entities.description: Liddell's edition transcribes the former Fitzwilliam manuscript now Duke Humfrey d. 2. Its Temporary Preface states that Add. A. 369 was used to correct the text where the base deviates from the original and that base readings are retained in notes. Printed pp. 259-289 append a systematic collation of Lodge's EETS edition with Add. A. 369. The title says Part I—Text; no later part is represented here.
entities.label: Mark Liddell, The Middle-English Translation of Palladius De Re Rustica, Part I—Text (1896)

rights.basis: The edition was published in 1896, and the official U-M item derived from it is marked Public Domain / No Copyright-United States.
rights.component: Liddell's 1896 edition text and editorial apparatus
notes: This textual-status statement is separate from Internet Archive's scan/OCR derivatives, whose exact item metadata supplies no license field.

access.accessMethod: Reference scans or higher-quality Imaging Services may be requested; the complete public IIIF should normally be evaluated first.
access.entityLabel: Bodleian Library MS. Duke Humfrey d. 2

entities.description: The carefully produced dedication copy to Humfrey, Duke of Gloucester, and Liddell's direct base. The manuscript contains the alphabetical index at fols. x r-xviii v and the work proper at fols. xix r-118v, with the prohemium at xix r-xxi v and the main translation restarting at 1r. Current catalog evidence dates production to 1439-1444 and leaves authorship uncertain.
entities.label: Bodleian Library MS. Duke Humfrey d. 2

physicalDescription.format: Codex; usually 28 written lines with blank lines between seven-line stanzas; main hand combines anglicana and secretary, with a second scribe at fols. 54-61
notes: Prohemium and book envois use multicolored script; the final envoy also uses gold.
The prohemium opens at fol. xix r; Book I opens at fol. 1r with Humfrey's arms.
Liddell's legacy Arabic foliation must not be confused with current Digital Bodleian labels.

entities.description: A 1440-1460 English parchment witness of the Middle English verse translation at fols. 14v-121v. It is Liddell's correction/collation witness and Lodge's direct base, not Liddell's base. It lacks a leaf in each of the first two quires, two leaves after fol. 92, part of fol. 121, the last 43 lines of Book XII, and all Book XIII.

holding.notes: Formerly at Colchester Castle; acquired by the Bodleian in 1886.

reviewNotes: The base-versus-collation distinction is explicit throughout: Duke Humfrey d. 2 is Liddell's direct base; Add. A. 369 supplies corrections and collation; Lodge's EETS edition is an additional appendix comparator.
Bodleian MS. Add. A. 369 is the former Colchester Castle manuscript. It is not a British Library manuscript, and the historical location is not treated as the current holding.
The current d.2 foliation is not a continuous x r-118v run: index x r-xviii v, prohemium xix r-xxi v, then main text 1r-118v. Liddell's legacy foliation remains edition evidence only.
The full d.2 object is UUID 9e046a1c-7f55-457e-8098-15ff757c0ee7 with 304 canvases. Same-title UUID a4593fe2-94d4-4f3e-9161-d2080bb35471 has only three selected canvases and is never called complete.

The complete cached Liddell provider PDF is complete for that scanned exemplar but does not include the U-M encoding's two unnumbered errata pages. Container completeness and edition-leaf coverage are therefore kept separate.

Rights remain layer-specific: U-M asserts NoC-US for its electronic item; exact Duke Humfrey d. 2 images and the public Add. A. 369 fol. 009v image are CC BY-NC 4.0, but that one Add. A. 369 image has zero target overlap; DIMEV v1.0 is CC BY 4.0; the inspected Internet Archive metadata provides no exact item license; any future complete or commissioned Add. A. 369 images require their own order-specific terms.
The generated dist/CME00114.pdf was inspected only as a repository viewer derivative and is not promoted into the historical source lineage.

lineage.summary: CME00114 is a repository copy of the University of Michigan encoding of Mark Liddell's 1896 edition. Liddell directly transcribed the dedication manuscript now Bodleian Library MS. Duke Humfrey d. 2; he used the later Bodleian MS. Add. A. 369 to correct and collate readings, and his appendix compares Barton Lodge's earlier EETS edition with that same Add. A. 369 witness. A complete 304-canvas Digital Bodleian facsimile of Duke Humfrey d. 2 is cached. The competing same-title three-canvas object is explicitly partial. No complete public page-image source for Add. A. 369 was found.

catalogSummary.author: Anonymous Middle English poet-translator
catalogSummary.date: Translated by 1444, probably close to production of the 1439-1444 dedication manuscript; exact composition date unresolved
catalogSummary.region: England; exact place of translation and dialect region unestablished
catalogSummary.title: Palladius on Husbondrie

catalogingSubject.label: Palladius on Husbondrie
catalogingSubject.scope_note: The cataloged subject is the anonymous Middle English verse translation of Palladius's Latin De re rustica, including its authorial prohemium and thirteen books. DIMEV indexes the prohemium separately as record 254 and the main poem as record 1071, but the dedication manuscript and Liddell's represented edition present them as one designed work. The manuscript's alphabetical Tabula is associated paratext, while Liddell's title page, appended collation, list of variations, and errata are modern edition layers; all are accounted in the source structure without being flattened into medieval authorship, work language, form, or subject facets.

contentParts.label: Liddell edition title page

locators.value: PDF p. 7; XML DIV1[1], PB REF 4

contentParts.summary: The modern title, editor, Part I—Text statement, Berlin publisher, and 1896 date.

contentParts.label: Alphabetical Tabula

locators.value: fols. x r-xviii v; complete IIIF canvases 21-38
DIV1[2], PB REF 10-27; 499 ITEM in 22 alphabetical DIV2 parts

notes: Associated manuscript paratext, not a second abstract work.
contentParts.summary: The dedication manuscript's extensive alphabetical subject index, transmitted before the prohemium and transcribed by Liddell.

contentParts.label: Prohemium: Agriculture as in Nature and Art

locators.value: fols. xix r-xxi v; complete IIIF canvases 39-44
DIV1[3], PB REF 28-32; 16 LG and 127 L

notes: The decorated opening line is HEAD[2], so the sixteen eight-line stanzas contain 128 represented line positions although only 127 are L elements.
DIMEV record 254 indexes this prohemium separately; it remains an integral part here.
contentParts.summary: A sixteen-stanza authorial prohemium invoking divine aid, addressing Humfrey, Duke of Gloucester, and framing the agricultural translation.

contentParts.label: Thirteen-book verse translation
notes: The current Bodleian foliation restarts at 1 after the prohemium; Liddell's legacy foliation numbers this run from 13.
contentParts.summary: The complete verse translation, beginning with agricultural foundations and continuing through twelve month-books from January to December.

contentParts.label: Book I: Aier, Watir, Lond, and Gouernaunce
contentParts.summary: Foundations of husbandry: air, water, land, buildings, estate disposition, livestock, tools, gardens, vines, and related management.

contentParts.label: Book XIII: Decembre

locators.value: DIV1[4]/DIV2[13], 7 chapters; DIV2/@N repeats 12 although HEAD says XIII

notes: Eight additional verse positions are encoded as a TABLE between numbered positions 70 and 79; the represented book has 86 verse positions.
contentParts.summary: Agricultural tasks for December and the poem's final devotional epilogue.

contentParts.label: Collation with MS. Bodl. Add. A. 369: Tabula

locators.value: DIV1[5], PB REF 268-278; 464 ITEM in 19 DIV2 parts

notes: Modern scholarly apparatus, not part of the medieval work.
contentParts.summary: Liddell's appended collation of the EETS edition with Bodleian MS. Add. A. 369, beginning with its Tabula variants.

contentParts.label: List of variations by book

locators.value: DIV1[6], PB REF 279-298; twelve DIV2 book headings and thirteen P

notes: The source edition's list has twelve DIV2 book divisions because Add. A. 369 lacks Book XIII.
contentParts.summary: Book-by-book readings and manuscript differences continuing Liddell's Add. A. 369 collation.

contentParts.label: Errata
notes: The cached Internet Archive scan ends after printed p. 289 and lacks these two unnumbered source pages; their exact exemplar remains unresolved.
contentParts.summary: Thirty-eight corrections to Liddell's text, notes, collation, and punctuation.

dateStatements.display: By 1444; probably close to production of the 1439-1444 dedication manuscript
notes: The current catalog securely dates manuscript production from a reference to Humfrey's Oxford donations. The dedication-copy context supports a 1444 terminus ante quem for the translation, but no inspected authorial datum proves 1439 as its terminus post quem.

dateStatements.display: 1439-1444 in Bodleian Library MS. Duke Humfrey d. 2
1873-1879 in Barton Lodge's Early English Text Society edition from MS. Add. A. 369
notes: The represented Duke Humfrey d. 2 edition is Liddell's later 1896 publication.

extent.description: Encoding metrics and modeled leaves: seven DIV1, sixty-six DIV2, 227 DIV3 (300 divisions total), 934 LG, 6,605 L, 292 PB, 2,015 NOTE, nineteen P, 1,002 ITEM, and one TABLE with eight ROW. Of the ITEM elements, 1,001 are content: 499 in the manuscript Tabula, 464 in the collation Tabula, and 38 in the errata; one additional ITEM is in TEMPHEAD/REVDESCR/CHANGE and is revision metadata. The nineteen counted content leaves below are the title page, manuscript Tabula, prohemium, thirteen books, collation Tabula, list of variations, and errata. Raw L count understates represented verse by nine positions: the decorated first prohemium line is a HEAD and Book XIII's eight-line verse table is encoded in eight ROW elements. Four fourteen-line LG elements each combine two seven-line stanzas. The represented verse therefore has 6,614 line positions and 939 stanza/table units, while viewer metrics preserve the auditable XML counts of 6,605 L and 934 LG.

formStatements.description: A verse translation predominantly in seven-line ababbcc rhyme-royal stanzas. The sixteen-stanza prohemium uses eight-line ababbcbc stanzas. Four main-text LG elements each combine two seven-line stanzas, and Book XIII's eight-line verse table is encoded as TABLE rows rather than L elements.

languageStatements.label: Middle English
notes: Latin rubrics and glosses belong to witness and editorial representation; modern English notes and apparatus belong to Liddell's edition. Neither layer makes the abstract translated work multilingual.
The primary path is repository XML to U-M encoding to Liddell's 1896 edition to Bodleian MS. Duke Humfrey d. 2. Add. A. 369 is a correction/collation witness, not Liddell's base.

Liddell's appendix additionally compares Barton Lodge's EETS edition with Add. A. 369; the edition title's 'formerly in Colchester Castle' describes that Bodleian witness, not a British Library manuscript.
Current foliation is split: Tabula x r-xviii v, prohemium xix r-xxi v, and main translation 1r-118v. Liddell's printed legacy foliation renumbers these runs and is preserved only as a crosswalk.

DIMEV record 254's separately indexed prohemium is modeled as an integral authorial part. Record 1071's twelve-book description conflicts with thirteen direct XML and printed headings; the direct edition governs.
Work discovery metrics remain raw encoding counts, with the nine non-L verse positions and four double-stanza LG elements disclosed rather than silently rewriting the XML.

The complete 304-canvas Digital Bodleian object is distinct from a competing three-canvas selected-image object with the same title; facsimile completeness is documented in the paired lineage manifest.
Rights, provider terms, complete-facsimile access, and reproduction routes are maintained layer by layer in the paired lineage manifest.

next_steps: Consult Howlett 1977 and subsequent responses rather than promoting the catalog's reported proposal to known authorship.
Compare linguistic evidence across Duke Humfrey d. 2, Add. A. 369, and other indexed witnesses while separating authorial language from scribal spelling.
Keep Humfrey's Oxford book donation, witness production in England, and composition geography as distinct claims.
openQuestions.question: Can the anonymous translator's identity, precise translation place, or dialect region be established beyond the current England context and the reported Thomas Norton proposal?

next_steps: Locate another complete physical or digital copy of Liddell 1896 and compare the leaves after printed p. 289.
Verify all thirty-eight encoded errata page by page against that exemplar.
Retain the cached Internet Archive scan as complete for its provider object but do not claim that it contains the errata leaves.
openQuestions.question: Which Liddell exemplar supplied the two unnumbered errata pages encoded as PB REF 299-300, and where can those pages be imaged directly?

next_steps: Do not treat the catalog-linked one-image Digital Bodleian object as target coverage: its sole canvas is fol. 009v, before the work begins at fol. 14v.
Ask Bodleian Special Collections or Imaging Services about existing photography or a complete imaging order and exact reuse terms.
Do not substitute Duke Humfrey d. 2 images for Add. A. 369 when evaluating Liddell's corrections or Lodge's transcript.
openQuestions.question: Can the target fols. 14v-121v of MS. Add. A. 369 be obtained as a complete usable facsimile?

notes: The current catalogs place both analyzed witnesses in England. Oxford is named in the prohemium as the recipient of Humfrey's books and is not silently promoted to a translation place.
Palladius is the author of the Latin source work, not of the Middle English wording or prohemium.
A reported scholarly proposal, not a normalized known authorship.
Edition-level responsibility only.

scope.description: Immediate 1896 source edition and its modern paratext

subjects.label: Seasonal rural practice
notes: Books II-XIII are organized from January through December; Book I treats foundational conditions and management.

summaries.value: An anonymous fifteenth-century Middle English verse translation of Palladius's late-antique agricultural treatise. An authorial prohemium dedicates the work to Humfrey, Duke of Gloucester; Book I treats air, water, land, buildings, and estate management, while Books II-XIII arrange agricultural tasks month by month. The poem covers soils, crops, vines, orchards, gardens, livestock, bees, farm structures, processing, storage, and preservation. CME00114 represents Mark Liddell's 1896 edition from the dedication manuscript now Bodleian MS. Duke Humfrey d. 2, corrected and collated against Bodleian MS. Add. A. 369, with Liddell's appended comparison to Barton Lodge's earlier EETS edition.

scope.description: Exact title of Mark Liddell's represented 1896 edition

titles.value: The Middle-English Translation of Palladius De Re Rustica
Middle English verse translation of Palladius's De re rustica

publication.embeddedTitle: THE MIDDLE-ENGLISH TRANSLATION OF PALLADIUS DE RE RUSTICA

work.regionDisplay: England; exact place of translation and dialect region unestablished
work.summary: An anonymous fifteenth-century Middle English verse translation of Palladius's late-antique agricultural treatise. An authorial prohemium dedicates the work to Humfrey, Duke of Gloucester; Book I treats air, water, land, buildings, and estate management, while Books II-XIII arrange agricultural tasks month by month. The poem covers soils, crops, vines, orchards, gardens, livestock, bees, farm structures, processing, storage, and preservation. CME00114 represents Mark Liddell's 1896 edition from the dedication manuscript now Bodleian MS. Duke Humfrey d. 2, corrected and collated against Bodleian MS. Add. A. 369, with Liddell's appended comparison to Barton Lodge's earlier EETS edition.
